title: NOC 22211 – Industrial Designers | GTR Immigration
description: Industrial Designers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by innovation in manufacturing, construction materials, consumer products, furniture design, and technology-driven product development. Classified under NOC 22211 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or university degree normally required

duties: Consult with clients and engineers on product requirements
Analyze user needs and functional applications
Research materials, costs, and production methods
Create CAD concepts, sketches, and 3-D models
employment_requirements: University degree in industrial design, architecture, or engineering or a college diploma in industrial design
Strong creative portfolio demonstrating design capability
Knowledge of CAD and prototyping tools
job_outlook: This competitive outlook favors candidates with:
additional_information: Canada’s design-driven manufacturing sector is actively recruiting in:
Consumer-goods manufacturingFurniture & interior-product companiesIndustrial-equipment producersConstruction-materials suppliersPrivate product-design studiosStart-ups and innovation labs
Annual salaries commonly range between $36,600–$87,600 CAD, depending on province, portfolio strength, and industry focus.
